fragility_points: A sharp BTC selloff before April 20 noon ET would compress the current cushion quickly.
The estimate is sensitive to short-horizon volatility because settlement turns on one exact minute close.
key_assumptions: Current ~5.7% cushion above $70k remains the dominant driver through April 20 absent a meaningful negative shock.
No hidden contract nuance materially changes the stated Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute final close rule.
main_logical_chain: Verify the governing source and exact contract mechanics: Binance BTC/USDT, 1-minute candle, April 20 12:00 ET, final Close > $70,000.
Check current Binance price surface and confirm BTC is materially above threshold several days early.
Treat current cushion as strong support for Yes but not near-certainty because the contract is close-specific rather than touch-based.
Mark probability below market because a single downside move at the exact observation minute can still resolve No.
main_thesis: Yes is favored because BTC is materially above $70k, but the market likely overprices certainty because settlement depends on one exact future Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at noon ET rather than any prior touch.
quote_anchors: the strongest credible variant view is that 0.87 is somewhat rich for a contract that settles on one exact Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close
All material conditions must hold simultaneously for Yes
source_quality_view: High-quality direct governing source verification, but evidence independence is only medium-low because the main sources are mechanically linked to the same settlement surface.
strongest_disconfirmers: The current cushion itself may justify a probability closer to the market's 0.87 than to 0.79.
Ordinary BTC volatility may still leave the exact April 20 noon ET close above $70k more often than this variant estimate assumes.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance spot checks show BTCUSDT around $73,995, comfortably above the $70,000 threshold.
There are only about five days left, so maintaining a multi-thousand-dollar cushion is more likely than not absent a sharp selloff.
timing_relevance: Highly date-sensitive: the market resolves on the exact April 20 noon ET 1-minute close, not on current spot or any earlier touch.
unresolved_ambiguities: The qualifying candle has not occurred yet, so the final governing-source proof cannot yet be captured.
UI candle labeling/time-conversion operational details are probably straightforward but still worth care at rerun near resolution.
what_would_change_view: I would move higher if BTC retains or expands its cushion into resolution; I would move lower if BTC falls back toward $70k or new risk events raise downside odds.
